data.title: UK Seeks Saudi Investment in Fighter Jet Program Amid Rising Costs
data.description: The United Kingdom is intensifying efforts to secure investment from Saudi Arabia for its next-generation fighter jet program, which is being developed in collaboration with Italy and Japan. Bloomberg posted on X that this move comes as the project faces escalating costs and a competing initiative experiences delays. The UK aims to bolster the program's financial backing and strengthen international partnerships in the defense sector. The collaboration with Saudi Arabia is seen as a strategic step to ensure the project's viability and success in the competitive global market.

data.title: Figure Launches FGRD Token with Instant Settlement and Lending Features
data.description: Figure has unveiled its FGRD token, representing common shares of the company directly issued on the blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this token provides instant settlement and incorporates built-in lending capabilities. To support this groundbreaking financial product, the company has expanded its funding round to $150 million.

data.title: Vale Sells Majority Stake in Canadian Nickel Venture
data.description: Vale has reached an agreement to sell the majority of its stake in a Canadian nickel venture to Exiro, Orion, and CGF. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of Vale's strategic review of its base metals business. The decision aligns with the company's broader efforts to optimize its portfolio and focus on core operations.

data.title: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange Group will launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options trading on May 29.
data.description: According to market sources, CME Group will launch 24/7 cryptocurrency futures and options trading on May 29.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Reduces Fees for Private Company Share Trading on EquityZen
data.description: Morgan Stanley has significantly lowered the fees for clients trading shares of private companies on its recently acquired EquityZen platform. Bloomberg posted on X that this move aims to attract more clients by offering competitive pricing, thereby challenging other firms in the market. The decision to cut costs by 50% is part of Morgan Stanley's strategy to expand its presence in the private equity trading sector. By reducing fees, the financial institution seeks to enhance its appeal to investors looking to trade private company shares, a market that has been gaining traction. This initiative reflects Morgan Stanley's commitment to providing cost-effective solutions and increasing its market share in the private equity space.

data.title: As Base migrates from the OP Stack to a proprietary technology stack, OP prices have fallen by over 20%.
data.description: The price of Optimism's native token, OP, has fallen by over 20% in the past 24 hours after Layer 2 solution Base announced its transition from the OP Stack to its self-running unified technology stack, base/base. Base stated that this move aims to accelerate development and reduce system complexity, and future node operators will need to migrate to the Base client to accommodate the hard fork. Base launched as the OP Stack chain in 2023, and according to a previous agreement, a portion of its sorter revenue was to be deposited into the Optimism treasury. With Base's shift to an independent architecture, this revenue stream will be retained by Base itself. Currently, OP is trading at $0.1436, a drop of approximately 97% from its all-time high of $4.84 in 2024. In contrast, Base has not yet issued any tokens.

data.title: The number of initial jobless claims in the U.S. this week was lower than expected.
data.description: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending February 14 was 206,000, the lowest since the week ending January 10, compared to market expectations of 225,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: India and Netherlands to Collaborate on AI and Digital Advancements
data.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with the Dutch Prime Minister during the AI Impact Summit, discussing significant progress in digital and artificial intelligence sectors. According to Jin10, the two leaders agreed to collaborate in these fields, focusing on strengthening ties in semiconductors, major water projects, green hydrogen, talent mobility, and counter-terrorism efforts.

data.title: Excluding Noncitizens from Census Could Impact Diverse Cities
data.description: The U.S. Census aims to count every individual residing in the country. Bloomberg posted on X that excluding noncitizens from this count could significantly affect America's largest and most racially diverse urban areas. This exclusion could lead to an underrepresentation of these communities, impacting federal funding and political representation. The debate continues as stakeholders consider the implications of such a policy change.

data.title: AGRICULTURE | U.S. Farmers to Increase Soybean Plantings Amid Export Revival to China
data.description: U.S. farmers are set to increase soybean plantings this spring while reducing corn cultivation, according to recent reports. Bloomberg posted on X that this shift comes as soybean exports to China have resumed after a period of stagnation. The revival of these exports is influencing planting decisions, with growers responding to the renewed demand from one of the world's largest soybean importers. Corn, traditionally the most widely grown crop in the United States, will see a reduction in planting as farmers adjust to market dynamics. This change reflects broader trends in agricultural trade and the impact of international demand on domestic farming strategies.

data.title: Bank of England's Mann Signals Extended Interest Rate Hold Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann has indicated her readiness to maintain interest rates at their current level for an extended period to manage inflation expectations. Bloomberg posted on X that Mann's stance comes despite her concerns about increasing unemployment rates. Mann emphasized the importance of controlling inflation to stabilize the economy, suggesting that a prolonged hold on interest rates might be necessary to achieve this goal. Her comments reflect the central bank's cautious approach in balancing economic growth with inflation control.

data.title: Tesla Evades California Sales Ban in Regulatory Victory
data.description:
Tesla has successfully circumvented a potential sales ban in California, marking a rare triumph for the electric vehicle manufacturer in regulatory matters. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's ability to navigate complex regulatory landscapes. The California Air Resources Board had considered imposing restrictions due to concerns over compliance with state emissions standards. However, Tesla's proactive measures and negotiations have allowed it to continue operations without interruption.

The decision comes amid increasing scrutiny of automotive emissions and environmental impact, with California often leading the charge in implementing stringent regulations. Tesla's ability to avoid the ban underscores its commitment to adhering to environmental standards while maintaining its market presence.

Industry experts note that this development could set a precedent for other automakers facing similar challenges in California and beyond. As the state continues to push for cleaner transportation solutions, companies like Tesla are under pressure to innovate and comply with evolving regulations.

Tesla's success in this instance may bolster its reputation as a leader in sustainable automotive technology, while also highlighting the importance of strategic regulatory engagement. The company remains focused on expanding its footprint in key markets, with California being a significant area of interest due to its size and environmental policies.

data.title: STOCKS | Wall Street's Focus on Carvana, Deere, Hims & Hers, and Walmart
data.description:
Wall Street is closely monitoring several companies ahead of the market opening. Bloomberg posted on X that Carvana, Deere, Hims & Hers, and Walmart are drawing significant attention from investors. Carvana, known for its online car sales platform, is under scrutiny as analysts assess its performance and future prospects. Deere, a major player in agricultural machinery, is also in the spotlight as market participants evaluate its earnings and market position.

Hims & Hers, a telehealth company, is gaining interest due to its innovative approach to healthcare services, which could impact its stock performance. Meanwhile, Walmart, the retail giant, continues to be a focal point for investors, with its strategies and financial results being closely analyzed.

These companies are expected to influence market dynamics as investors weigh their potential impact on stock prices and overall market trends.

data.title: Ares to Expand Sports Investment Strategy in Europe
data.description: Ares Management Corp is set to broaden its sports investment strategy to affluent European investors following its successful launch in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, citing a source familiar with the situation, that the firm aims to capitalize on the growing interest in sports investments among wealthy individuals in Europe. This expansion comes after Ares introduced the strategy in the U.S., where it has garnered significant attention and interest. The move is part of Ares' broader plan to enhance its offerings and tap into new markets, leveraging its expertise in sports investments to attract high-net-worth clients across Europe.

data.title: Crypto Investors Face Rising Threats of Physical Attacks
data.description: Physical attacks against cryptocurrency investors are increasing, with incidents involving severe violence such as finger amputations. According to NS3.AI, these violent 'wrench attacks' are becoming more common, prompting experts to advise on self-defense and situational awareness. Tom Matthews, a former Secret Service member now with Asymmetric Security, emphasizes the importance of simple self-defense techniques to counter these threats. The industry is urged to implement policies for kidnapping and ransom negotiation and enhance online security through privacy tools and careful management of crypto wallets.

data.title: Strategies for Navigating Prolonged Crypto Bear Markets
data.description: Arkham has identified six strategies to help investors navigate and potentially profit during extended crypto bear markets, characterized by asset price declines of 20% or more and heightened volatility. According to NS3.AI, these strategies include short selling, utilizing options and inverse products, engaging in range trading, accumulating quality assets, employing stablecoin yield strategies, and practicing scalping or day trading. The report highlights the importance of disciplined risk management and warns of potential risks such as liquidity challenges and emotional pressures during bear markets.

data.title: Bundesbank President Advocates Euro-Denominated Stablecoins Amid Geopolitical Concerns
data.description:
Joachim Nagel, President of the German Bundesbank, has expressed support for the development of euro-denominated stablecoins. According to NS3.AI, these stablecoins are seen as a means to provide low-cost cross-border payments and mitigate the risk of dollarization from USD-pegged stablecoins. The European Central Bank (ECB) is currently exploring both wholesale Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) and euro-pegged stablecoins as tools to uphold monetary policy effectiveness and European sovereignty in the face of geopolitical uncertainties.

While stablecoins offer several advantages, experts have raised concerns about potential risks such as illicit use and disruptions to monetary policy. As a result, tokenized deposits are being considered as a potentially safer alternative. The ongoing exploration by the ECB reflects a broader effort to adapt to evolving financial landscapes and ensure stability in the European monetary system.

data.title: The head of the German Federal Bank wants to introduce a stablecoin pegged to the euro to prevent dollarization.
data.description: Joachim Nagel, a member of the Governing Council of the European Central Bank and President of the Bundesbank, stated in a speech at the American Chamber of Commerce in Germany that the introduction of a euro-denominated stablecoin could provide individuals and businesses with low-cost cross-border payment services and address the dollarization risks posed by dollar-denominated stablecoins. Nagel pointed out that if domestic currencies are replaced by dollar-denominated stablecoins, it would be tantamount to dollarization of the relevant economies, potentially severely undermining the effectiveness of domestic monetary policy and European sovereignty. Currently, the European Central Bank is assessing the possibility of using distributed ledgers in non-central bank currencies, including tokenized deposits and euro stablecoins, and plans to launch a digital euro in 2029. Furthermore, the European Central Bank is also developing a wholesale CBDC to allow financial market participants to execute programmable transactions.

data.title: Representatives from Coinbase, Ripple, and a16z Crypto attended the stablecoin yield conference.
data.description: Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that attendees at the third meeting on stablecoin yields included Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal, Ripple Chief Legal Officer Stuart Alderoty, a16z Crypto Policy Director Miles Jennings, and other banking representatives. The meeting was held at the White House at 9:00 AM Eastern Time on February 19th and was attended by representatives from the cryptocurrency industry and the banking sector.

data.title: Oil Supermajors Seek New Opportunities Beyond Guyana
data.description: Oil supermajors are actively searching for new exploration opportunities similar to Guyana's recent success. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges these companies face in finding new lucrative oil fields. The search for the next big discovery is driven by the need to replace depleting reserves and capitalize on rising energy demands. However, identifying regions with similar potential to Guyana is proving difficult due to geopolitical, environmental, and technical challenges. As the industry evolves, companies are focusing on innovative strategies and technologies to uncover new resources and maintain their competitive edge in the global market.
